title: Execute a SOAPMessageV2 call from script
question: Write a server-side script that executes a configured SOAP message and logs the status code.
summary: Adds direct coding coverage for SOAP integrations.
exactAnswer: Instantiate the configured SOAP message record, execute it, and log the HTTP status so you can validate connectivity before parsing the full payload.
steps: Create the SOAP message and method first.
Open Scripts - Background or a Script Include test harness.
Log the status and inspect the body if needed.
expectedOutput: A log entry shows the SOAP response status code.
commonMistakes: Executing a SOAP message before configuring auth and endpoint details.
explanation: This is the fastest safe way to validate the transport before adding payload parsing.

title: Condition script for a mobile action
question: Write a script condition that shows a mobile action only when the incident is active and assigned to the current user.
summary: Adds coding coverage for the Mobile module.
exactAnswer: Use a short server-side condition that checks the active state and current assignee so the action only appears when it is contextually useful to the technician.
steps: Place the logic in the condition script area.
Test with an assigned and unassigned active incident.
expectedOutput: The mobile action appears only for the assigned active incident context.
commonMistakes: Using display values in a sys_id comparison.
explanation: Small condition scripts keep mobile actions predictable and easy to support.

title: Prepare a simple decision output for Virtual Agent handoff
question: Write a server-side snippet that returns a support queue label based on incident priority for a Virtual Agent decision.
summary: Adds coding coverage for Virtual Agent related logic.
exactAnswer: Return a simple string outcome that a Virtual Agent topic or flow can use to route the user toward the right handoff path.
steps: Open Scripts - Background for a quick validation.
Paste the snippet with a sample priority variable.
Use the same idea in your Virtual Agent action or decision logic.
commonMistakes: Embedding large routing trees directly into a topic when a decision table would scale better.
explanation: Keep chat-routing logic small unless it truly belongs in a reusable decision model.

title: Validate a deployment window before DevOps promotion
question: Write a script that logs whether the current time is inside a permitted deployment window.
summary: Adds coding coverage for DevOps and release governance.
exactAnswer: Capture the current hour and compare it to the approved deployment window before allowing the promotion logic to continue.
steps: Run the check with your chosen time window.
Move the same pattern into gated promotion logic if needed.
expectedOutput: Deployment window open: true or false
commonMistakes: Hardcoding promotion timing with no visible check or logging.
explanation: Even a small gate improves governance when tied to release automation.
